title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple discusses her theory with Detective Dermot Craddock and Detective Dermot Coddle regarding the children of Marina Gregg. She suggests that the children, who were sent away after enjoying luxuries, may have developed resentment due to feelings of rejection. She expresses interest in discovering their current ages and locations, noting that magazines suggest they are now adults.

Craddock reviews a list of potential suspects. He notes that Jason Rad, Marina's husband, is a prime suspect because he attempted to hide that his wife was the intended victim to avoid terrifying her. Marina is described as neurotic and prone to anxiety attacks. Craddock also mentions Ella Jerinsky, the efficient secretary, and explores the possibility of a motive involving her feelings for Marina's husband. Other individuals mentioned include local photographers, newspaper reporters, a former wife of Marina's husband, and Adwick Fenn, a former close friend whose unexpected appearance caused a stir. Additionally, a young man named Blackie Preston is noted for being evasive about the events of the day.

In a separate discussion, Inspector Cornish and Craddock evaluate the suspects present on the landing when Marina Gregg displayed a "frozen expression." While Cornish briefly considers the corrupt mayor, he is ruled out. The Glass couple is also excluded as they never left St. Mary Mead. The remaining suspects include the American stranger Adwick Fenn, the American film star Laura Bruster—the ex-wife of Marina's third husband—and a London photographer named Margaret Bens. Craddock decides to interview the reporter and travel to London to investigate Fenn, Bruster, and Bens.
